items.headline: Wall Street Ends Week Lower Amid Inflation and AI Concerns
items.summary: U.S. equities concluded Friday, February 28, 2026, with a significant slide, capping a challenging week. Concerns over hotter-than-expected inflation data, particularly the January Producer Price Index, intensified stagflation fears among traders. Adding to the market's unease were ongoing anxieties about the disruptive impact of artificial intelligence on employment, exemplified by major corporate workforce reductions. The major indices, including the S&P 500, Nasdaq, and Dow, all posted declines for the day and the week.

items.headline: Circle Internet Group Surges on Strong Q4
items.summary: Circle Internet Group ($CRCL) saw a significant weekly gain of +32.40% to $83.44, driven by better-than-expected Q4 earnings and revenue, with USDC in circulation climbing 72% year-over-year.

items.headline: Block Soars on AI-Driven Workforce Cut
items.summary: Payments firm Block ($SQ) jumped 16.8% on Friday after announcing plans to cut nearly half its workforce, citing AI tools enabling a significantly smaller team to achieve more.

items.headline: Netflix Jumps After Dropping Warner Bros. Bid
items.summary: Netflix ($NFLX) climbed 13.8% on Friday after abandoning its bid for Warner Bros. Discovery's studio and streaming business.

items.headline: Amphastar Plunges on Q4 Earnings Miss
items.summary: Amphastar Pharmaceuticals ($AMPH) plummeted 24% on Friday following its Q4 2025 earnings report, which significantly missed profitability and revenue estimates and offered weak 2026 guidance.

items.headline: NVIDIA Slumps Despite Record Earnings
items.summary: Despite reporting a 'historic beat' for fiscal Q4 2026 earnings with record revenue and strong guidance, NVIDIA ($NVDA) fell approximately 10% over two days. Investor skepticism about sustaining elevated growth and broader 'AI jitters' weighed on the stock.

items.headline: Nu Holdings Declines Despite Record Revenue
items.summary: Nu Holdings ($NU) dropped 14.55% week-over-week to $14.98. While the digital banking platform reported record Q4 revenue that exceeded estimates, a decrease in its efficiency ratio led to the decline.

items.headline: Wholesale Inflation Surges, Fueling Rate Cut Concerns
items.summary: The January Producer Price Index (PPI) report, released on February 27, showed headline producer prices rising 0.5% month-over-month, with core PPI surging 0.8%—the hottest reading in over a year. This unexpectedly high inflation data pressured market expectations for future rate cuts and contributed to the broad market selloff.

items.headline: AI Job Displacement Fears Grips Wall Street
items.summary: Concerns over AI's impact on the workforce intensified after companies like Block announced significant job cuts, attributing them to the capabilities of AI tools. This trend is sparking fears about potential widespread job displacement and its broader economic implications.

items.headline: Market Sees Rotation from Growth to Value
items.summary: The dominant market narrative for the week was a rotation from growth to value stocks. The 'Magnificent Seven' tech giants experienced declines year-to-date, while sectors like Consumer Staples and Energy outperformed, signaling a shift in investor preference. The iShares Expanded Tech-Software ETF alone lost nearly 10% in February.

items.headline: Q4 2025 GDP Misses, Labor Market Holds Firm
items.summary: Fourth-quarter 2025 GDP grew at an annualized rate of 1.4%, missing consensus estimates of 2.5%, partly due to a government shutdown. Despite this, the labor market showed resilience, with initial jobless claims coming in below estimates and continuing claims dropping.

items.headline: Berkshire Hathaway's Q4 Operating Earnings Decline
items.summary: Berkshire Hathaway ($BRK.A, $BRK.B) reported a 30% year-over-year drop in Q4 operating earnings, although its cash pile reached $373.3 billion. The report was released over the weekend, setting a tone for discussions on corporate America's health.

items.headline: Consumer Confidence Improves Slightly in February
items.summary: The Conference Board Consumer Confidence Index edged up to 91.2 in February, a 2.2-point increase. The University of Michigan's final February consumer sentiment reading was 56.6, slightly down from preliminary figures but up from January. One-year inflation expectations also saw a decrease.
